title: Fewer than 3 tests — only a trivial placeholder test exists
evidence: src/lib.rs:11 `it_works` asserts add(2,2)==4; tests/ is empty; no test exercises any book route or DB call. test_coverage=1.0 only reflects 1/1 trivial test passing.
suggestion: Add >=3 integration tests hitting the CRUD/health routes (e.g. via actix_web::test) that assert status codes and JSON bodies.

title: ?author= filter uses the wrong extractor and does not work
evidence: src/main.rs:64 `author: web::Query<Option<String>>` — serde_urlencoded cannot bind the `author` key into `Option<String>`; `GET /books?author=X` fails Query extraction (400) rather than filtering. Only the no-param list path yields None and works.
suggestion: Deserialize into a struct, e.g. `#[derive(Deserialize)] struct BookQuery { author: Option<String> }` and pass `web::Query<BookQuery>`.

title: SQLite connect string lacks create-if-missing; server fails to start on a fresh checkout
evidence: src/main.rs:217 and src/main.rs:36/65/94/144/178 use `sqlite://books.db` with sqlx default create_if_missing=false. No books.db ships in the run_dir, so `SqlitePool::connect` errors and main() calls process::exit(1) before binding.

title: New DB pool opened per request; shared web::Data<SqlitePool> is never used
evidence: src/main.rs:36,65,94,144,178 each call Database::new(...) (opening a pool and re-running CREATE TABLE) on every request; the injected `db: web::Data<SqlitePool>` parameter is ignored in all handlers.
suggestion: Store the Database (or pool) in web::Data once at startup and use the injected handle in every handler.

title: Stray placeholder crate book_api/ left in the workspace
evidence: book_api/src/main.rs:1 is `fn main(){ println!("Hello, world!"); }` with an empty-dependency book_api/Cargo.toml — dead scaffolding duplicating the real crate name.

title: Manual smoke script posts to a malformed host
evidence: test_api.sh:17 targets `http://127.0.1:8080/books` (missing an octet) instead of 127.0.0.1, so the POST in the smoke test never reaches the server.
